output.dependency: A trip/route code (e.g., origin-destination and airline) determines which departure time value appears; the departure-time text is tied to the specific route/itinerary shown by the route identifier.
A trip/route code determines which first segment’s associated time value appears; the earlier time text is tied to the specific route/itinerary represented by the route identifier.
A trip/route code determines which later time value appears (e.g., subsequent segment/arrival time); the time text follows the schedule implied by the route identifier.
A trip/route code determines which final/other time value appears; the time text is part of the schedule pattern for the itinerary encoded in the route identifier.

Two time columns appear to be paired for a given itinerary (e.g., departure/arrival or start/connection). Their values are consistent with a fixed ordering and spacing implied by the trip schedule format.
Two later time columns appear to be paired (e.g., destination-side times). Their values follow the same schedule structure and are not independent; they belong to the same itinerary timeframe.
The booking/source/provider label likely determines which route codes are available/selected; the route identifier corresponds to the itinerary for that provider.
